You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README
[![NPM Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/pyodide)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/pyodide) [![PyPI Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pyodide-py.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/pyodide-py/) [![Build Status](https://circleci.com/gh/pyodide/pyodide.png)](https://circleci.com/gh/pyodide/pyodide) [![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/pyodide/badge/?version=stable)](https://pyodide.readthedocs.io/?badge=stable) Pyodide is a Python distribution for the browser and Node.js based on WebAssembly. ## What is Pyodide? Pyodide is a port of CPython to WebAssembly/[Emscripten](https://emscripten.org/). Pyodide makes it possible to install and run Python packages in the browser with [micropip](https://micropip.pyodide.org/). Any pure Python package with a wheel available on PyPi is supported. Many packages with C, C++, and Rust extensions have also been ported for use with Pyodide. These include many general-purpose packages such as regex, PyYAML, and cryptography, and scientific Python packages including NumPy, pandas, SciPy, Matplotlib, and scikit-learn. Pyodide comes with a robust Javascript ⟺ Python foreign function interface so that you can freely mix these two languages in your code with minimal friction. This includes full support for error handling, async/await, and much more. When used inside a browser, Python has full access to the Web APIs. ## Try Pyodide (no installation needed) Try Pyodide in a [REPL](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/console.html) directly in your browser. For further information, see the [documentation](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/). ## Getting Started - If you wish to use a hosted distribution of Pyodide: see the [Getting Started](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/usage/quickstart.html) documentation. - If you wish to host Pyodide yourself, you can download Pyodide from the [releases page](https://github.com/pyodide/pyodide/releases/) and serve it with a web server. - If you wish to use Pyodide with a bundler, see [the documentation on Working with Bundlers](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/usage/working-with-bundlers.html) - If you are a Python package maintainer, see [the documentation on building and testing Python packages](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/development/building-and-testing-packages.html). - If you want to add a package to the Pyodide distribution, [see the documentation on adding a package to the Pyodide distribution](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/development/new-packages.html) - If you wish to experiment or contribute back to the Pyodide runtime, see the documentation on [building Pyodide from source](https://pyodide.org/en/stable/development/building-from-sources.html) ## History Pyodide was created in 2018 by [Michael Droettboom](https://github.com/mdboom) at Mozilla as part of the [Iodide project](http ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods

import sys
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory

import pytest
from pytest_pyodide import run_in_pyodide, spawn_web_server

from conftest import package_is_built

cpver = f"cp{sys.version_info.major}{sys.version_info.minor}"


WHEEL_BASE = None


@pytest.fixture
def wheel_base(monkeypatch):
    with TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdirname:
        global WHEEL_BASE
        WHEEL_BASE = Path(tmpdirname).absolute()
        import site

        monkeypatch.setattr(
            site, "getsitepackages", lambda: [WHEEL_BASE], raising=False
        )
        try:
            yield
        finally:
            WHEEL_BASE = None


@pytest.fixture
def selenium_standalone_micropip(selenium_standalone):
    """Import micropip before entering test so that global initialization of
    micropip doesn't count towards hiwire refcount.
    """
    selenium_standalone.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.loadPackage("micropip");
        pyodide.runPython("import micropip");
        """
    )
    yield selenium_standalone


SNOWBALL_WHEEL = "snowballstemmer-2.0.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl"


def test_install_simple(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    assert selenium.run_js(
        """
            return await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import os
                import micropip
                from pyodide.ffi import to_js
                # Package 'pyodide-micropip-test' has dependency on 'snowballstemmer'
                # It is used to test markers support
                await micropip.install('pyodide-micropip-test')
                import snowballstemmer
                stemmer = snowballstemmer.stemmer('english')
                to_js(stemmer.stemWords('go going goes gone'.split()))
            `);
            """
    ) == ["go", "go", "goe", "gone"]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("base_url", ["'{base_url}'", "'.'"])
def test_install_custom_url(selenium_standalone_micropip, base_url):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip

    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:
        server_hostname, server_port, _ = server
        base_url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"
        url = base_url + SNOWBALL_WHEEL

        selenium.run_js(
            f"""
            await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import micropip
                await micropip.install('{url}')
                import snowballstemmer
            `);
            """
        )


@pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(chrome="node only", firefox="node only")
def test_install_file_protocol_node(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    from conftest import DIST_PATH

    pyparsing_wheel_name = list(DIST_PATH.glob("pyparsing*.whl"))[0].name
    selenium.run_js(
        f"""
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install('file:{pyparsing_wheel_name}')
            import pyparsing
        `);
        """
    )


def test_install_different_version(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install(
                "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/89/06/2c2d3034b4d6bf22f2a4ae546d16925898658a33b4400cfb7e2c1e2871a3/pytz-2020.5-py2.py3-none-any.whl"
            );
        `);
        """
    )
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import pytz
            assert pytz.__version__ == "2020.5"
        `);
        """
    )


def test_install_different_version2(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install(
                "pytz == 2020.5"
            );
        `);
        """
    )
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import pytz
            assert pytz.__version__ == "2020.5"
        `);
        """
    )


@pytest.mark.parametrize("jinja2", ["jinja2", "Jinja2"])
def test_install_mixed_case2(selenium_standalone_micropip, jinja2):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        f"""
        await pyodide.loadPackage("micropip");
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install("{jinja2}")
            import jinja2
        `);
        """
    )


def test_list_load_package_from_url(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:
        server_hostname, server_port, _ = server
        base_url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"
        url = base_url + SNOWBALL_WHEEL

        selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
        selenium.run_js(

            await pyodide.loadPackage({url!r});
            await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import micropip
                assert "snowballstemmer" in micropip.list()
            `);
            """
        )


def test_list_pyodide_package(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(

        `);

def test_list_loaded_from_js(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.loadPackage("regex");

            import micropip
            pkgs = micropip.list()
            assert "regex" in pkgs
            assert pkgs["regex"].source.lower() == "pyodide"
        `);
        """
    )


def test_emfs(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:
        server_hostname, server_port, _ = server
        url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"

        @run_in_pyodide(packages=["micropip"])
        async def run_test(selenium, url, wheel_name):
            import micropip
            from pyodide.http import pyfetch

            resp = await pyfetch(url + wheel_name)
            await resp._into_file(open(wheel_name, "wb"))
            await micropip.install("emfs:" + wheel_name)
            import snowballstemmer

            stemmer = snowballstemmer.stemmer("english")
            assert stemmer.stemWords("go going goes gone".split()) == [
                "go",
                "go",
                "goe",
                "gone",
            ]

        run_test(selenium_standalone_micropip, url, SNOWBALL_WHEEL)


def test_install_non_normalized_package(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    if not package_is_built("ruamel-yaml"):
        pytest.skip("ruamel.yaml not built")

    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip

    selenium.run_async(
        """

        await micropip.install("ruamel.yaml")

    )

### File Overview
This test module exercises micropip-related functionality in pyodide (installing wheels from various sources, listing installed packages, and special protocols). It uses Selenium-driven JS to run pyodide Python code and an embedded test web server to serve wheel files.

### Architecture
- Module-level constants: `cpver`, `WHEEL_BASE`, and `SNOWBALL_WHEEL` configure environment and test assets.
- Pytest fixtures:
  - `wheel_base` monkeypatches site.getsitepackages to a temporary directory and controls the `WHEEL_BASE` global.
  - `selenium_standalone_micropip` ensures `micropip` is imported/loaded in pyodide before tests run (so global initialization is not counted toward hiwire refcount).
- Tests mostly drive pyodide by calling Selenium's `run_js`/`run_async`/`run_async`-style helpers to evaluate Python snippets with `pyodide.runPythonAsync` or `pyodide.loadPackage`.
- `spawn_web_server` (imported from pytest_pyodide) is used to host wheel files from the local test directory during tests.
- One test (`test_emfs`) uses the `run_in_pyodide` decorator to run a Python coroutine directly with pyodide and exercises `pyodide.http.pyfetch` and the `emfs:` protocol.
- Tests assert correct behavior by importing modules after installing or by inspecting the return value of micropip APIs (e.g., `micropip.list()`).

### Functions & Classes

import sys
from pathlib import Path
from tempfile import TemporaryDirectory

import pytest
from pytest_pyodide import run_in_pyodide, spawn_web_server

from conftest import package_is_built

cpver = f"cp{sys.version_info.major}{sys.version_info.minor}"


WHEEL_BASE = None


@pytest.fixture
def wheel_base(monkeypatch):
    with TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdirname:
        global WHEEL_BASE
        WHEEL_BASE = Path(tmpdirname).absolute()
        import site

        monkeypatch.setattr(
            site, "getsitepackages", lambda: [WHEEL_BASE], raising=False
        )
        try:
            yield
        finally:
            WHEEL_BASE = None


@pytest.fixture
def selenium_standalone_micropip(selenium_standalone):
    """Import micropip before entering test so that global initialization of
    micropip doesn't count towards hiwire refcount.
    """
    selenium_standalone.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.loadPackage("micropip");
        pyodide.runPython("import micropip");
        """
    )
    yield selenium_standalone


SNOWBALL_WHEEL = "snowballstemmer-2.0.0-py2.py3-none-any.whl"


def test_install_simple(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    assert selenium.run_js(
        """
            return await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import os
                import micropip
                from pyodide.ffi import to_js
                # Package 'pyodide-micropip-test' has dependency on 'snowballstemmer'
                # It is used to test markers support
                await micropip.install('pyodide-micropip-test')
                import snowballstemmer
                stemmer = snowballstemmer.stemmer('english')
                to_js(stemmer.stemWords('go going goes gone'.split()))
            `);
            """
    ) == ["go", "go", "goe", "gone"]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("base_url", ["'{base_url}'", "'.'"])
def test_install_custom_url(selenium_standalone_micropip, base_url):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip

    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:
        server_hostname, server_port, _ = server
        base_url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"
        url = base_url + SNOWBALL_WHEEL

        selenium.run_js(
            f"""
            await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import micropip
                await micropip.install('{url}')
                import snowballstemmer
            `);
            """
        )


@pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(chrome="node only", firefox="node only")
def test_install_file_protocol_node(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    from conftest import DIST_PATH

    pyparsing_wheel_name = list(DIST_PATH.glob("pyparsing*.whl"))[0].name
    selenium.run_js(
        f"""
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install('file:{pyparsing_wheel_name}')
            import pyparsing
        `);

    )


def test_install_different_version(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install(
                "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/89/06/2c2d3034b4d6bf22f2a4ae546d16925898658a33b4400cfb7e2c1e2871a3/pytz-2020.5-py2.py3-none-any.whl"
            );

def test_install_mixed_case2(selenium_standalone_micropip, jinja2):

    selenium.run_js(

def test_list_load_package_from_url(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:

        base_url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"
        url = base_url + SNOWBALL_WHEEL

        selenium.run_js(

            await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
                import micropip
                assert "snowballstemmer" in micropip.list()
            `);
            """
        )


def test_list_pyodide_package(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            await micropip.install(
                "regex"
            );
        `);
        """
    )
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            pkgs = micropip.list()
            assert "regex" in pkgs
            assert pkgs["regex"].source.lower() == "pyodide"
        `);
        """
    )


def test_list_loaded_from_js(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip
    selenium.run_js(
        """
        await pyodide.loadPackage("regex");
        await pyodide.runPythonAsync(`
            import micropip
            pkgs = micropip.list()
            assert "regex" in pkgs
            assert pkgs["regex"].source.lower() == "pyodide"
        `);
        """
    )


def test_emfs(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    with spawn_web_server(Path(__file__).parent / "test") as server:
        server_hostname, server_port, _ = server
        url = f"http://{server_hostname}:{server_port}/"

        @run_in_pyodide(packages=["micropip"])
        async def run_test(selenium, url, wheel_name):
            import micropip
            from pyodide.http import pyfetch

            resp = await pyfetch(url + wheel_name)
            await resp._into_file(open(wheel_name, "wb"))
            await micropip.install("emfs:" + wheel_name)
            import snowballstemmer

            stemmer = snowballstemmer.stemmer("english")
            assert stemmer.stemWords("go going goes gone".split()) == [
                "go",
                "go",
                "goe",
                "gone",
            ]

        run_test(selenium_standalone_micropip, url, SNOWBALL_WHEEL)


def test_install_non_normalized_package(selenium_standalone_micropip):
    if not package_is_built("ruamel-yaml"):
        pytest.skip("ruamel.yaml not built")

    selenium = selenium_standalone_micropip

    selenium.run_async(
        """
        import micropip
        await micropip.install("ruamel.yaml")
        import ruamel.yaml
        """
    )

import re
import shutil
import sys
from collections.abc import Iterable
from importlib.machinery import EXTENSION_SUFFIXES
from pathlib import Path
from site import getsitepackages
from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile
from typing import IO, Any, Literal
from zipfile import ZipFile

try:
    from pyodide_js import loadedPackages
except ImportError:
    loadedPackages = None

from .common import install_files
from .ffi import IN_BROWSER, JsArray, JsBuffer, to_js

SITE_PACKAGES = Path(getsitepackages()[0])
if sys.base_prefix == sys.prefix:
    # not in a virtualenv
    DSO_DIR = Path("/usr/lib")
else:
    # in a virtualenv
    # Better not put stuff into /usr/lib or /lib/python3.10! Stick the stdlib
    # into SITE_PACKAGES and dsos up two directories.
    #
    # e.g., SITE_PACKAGES = .venv/lib/python3.10/site_packages
    # and   DSO_DIR       = .venv/lib/
    DSO_DIR = SITE_PACKAGES.parents[1]
TARGETS = {"site": SITE_PACKAGES, "dynlib": DSO_DIR}

ZIP_TYPES = {".whl", ".zip"}
TAR_TYPES = {
    ".bz",
    ".bz2",
    ".tbz2",
    ".gz",
    ".tgz",
    ".tar",
}
EXTENSION_TAGS = [suffix.removesuffix(".so") for suffix in EXTENSION_SUFFIXES]
# See PEP 3149. I think the situation has since been updated since PEP 3149 does
# not talk about platform triples. But I could not find any newer pep discussing
# shared library names.
#
# There are other interpreters but it's better to have false negatives than
# false positives.
PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX = re.compile(
    r"\.(cpython|pypy|jython)-[0-9]{2,}[a-z]*(-[a-z0-9_-]*)?"
)
SHAREDLIB_REGEX = re.compile(r"\.so(.\d+)*$")

DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX = ".dist-info"
DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX = ".data"
# There are other "scheme"s available, but we are not interested in them.
# https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/81041f7f573e89361e6ed934436adb6bf40ea3bc/src/pip/_internal/models/scheme.py#L10
DATA_FILES_SCHEME = "data"

PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE = "PYODIDE_SOURCE"


def parse_wheel_name(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str, str, str, str]:
    tokens = filename.split("-")
    # TODO: support optional build tags in the filename (cf PEP 427)
    if len(tokens) < 5:
        raise ValueError(f"{filename} is not a valid wheel file name.")
    version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform = tokens[-4:]
    name = "-".join(tokens[:-4])
    return name, version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform


# Vendored from packaging
_canonicalize_regex = re.compile(r"[-_.]+")


def canonicalize_name(name: str) -> str:
    # This is taken from PEP 503.
    return _canonicalize_regex.sub("-", name).lower()


# Vendored from pip
class UnsupportedWheel(Exception):
    """Unsupported wheel."""


def find_wheel_metadata_dir(source: ZipFile, suffix: str) -> str | None:
    """
    Returns the name of the contained metadata directory inside the wheel file.

    Parameters
    ----------
    source
        A ZipFile object representing the wheel file.

    suffix
        The suffix of the metadata directory. Usually ".dist-info" or ".data"

    Returns
    -------
        The name of the metadata directory. If not found, returns None.
    """

    # Zip file path separators must be /
    subdirs = {p.split("/", 1)[0] for p in source.namelist()}

    info_dirs = [s for s in subdirs if s.endswith(suffix)]

    if not info_dirs:
        return None

    # Choose the first directory if there are multiple directories
    info_dir = info_dirs[0]
    return info_dir


def wheel_dist_info_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str:
    """
    Returns the name of the contained .dist-info directory.
    """
    dist_info_dir = find_wheel_metadata_dir(source, suffix=DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX)
    if dist_info_dir is None:
        raise UnsupportedWheel(
            f"{DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX} directory not found in wheel {name!r}"
        )

    dist_info_dir_name = canonicalize_name(dist_info_dir)
    canonical_name = canonicalize_name(name)
    if not dist_info_dir_name.startswith(canonical_name):
        raise UnsupportedWheel(
            f"{DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX} directory {dist_info_dir!r} does not start with {canonical_name!r}"
        )

    return dist_info_dir


def wheel_data_file_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str | None:
    data_file_dir = find_wheel_metadata_dir(source, suffix=DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX)

    # data files are optional, so we return None if not found
    if data_file_dir is None:
        return None

    data_file_dir_name = canonicalize_name(data_file_dir)
    canonical_name = canonicalize_name(name)
    if not data_file_dir_name.startswith(canonical_name):
        return None

    return data_file_dir


def make_whlfile(
    *args: Any, owner: int | None = None, group: int | None = None, **kwargs: Any
) -> str:
    return shutil._make_zipfile(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]


if IN_BROWSER:
    shutil.register_archive_format("whl", make_whlfile, description="Wheel file")
    shutil.register_unpack_format(
        "whl",
        [".whl", ".wheel"],
        shutil._unpack_zipfile,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        description="Wheel file",
    )


def get_format(format: str) -> str:
    for fmt, extensions, _ in shutil.get_unpack_formats():
        if format == fmt:
            return fmt
        if format in extensions:
            return fmt
        if "." + format in extensions:
            return fmt
    raise ValueError(f"Unrecognized format {format}")


def get_install_dir(target: Literal["site", "dynlib"] | None = None) -> str:
    """
    Get the installation directory for a target.
    """
    if not target:
        return str(SITE_PACKAGES)

    return str(TARGETS.get(target, SITE_PACKAGES))


def unpack_buffer(
    buffer: JsBuffer,
    *,
    filename: str = "",
    format: str | None = None,
    extract_dir: str | None = None,
    calculate_dynlibs: bool = False,
    metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> JsArray[str] | None:
    """Used to install a package either into sitepackages or into the standard
    library.

    This is a helper method called from ``loadPackage``.

    Parameters
    ----------
    buffer
        A Javascript ``Uint8Array`` with the binary data for the archive.

    filename
        The name of the file we are extracting. We only care about it to figure
        out whether the buffer represents a tar file or a zip file. Ignored if
        format argument is present.

    format
        Controls the format that we assume the archive has. Overrides the file
        extension of filename. In particular we decide the file format as
        follows:

        1. If format is present, we use that.
        2. If file name is present, it should have an extension, like a.zip,
           a.tar, etc. Then we use that.
        3. If neither is present or the file name has no extension, we throw an
           error.

    extract_dir
        Controls which directory the file is unpacked into. Default is the
        working directory.

    calculate_dynlibs
        If true, will return a Javascript Array of paths to dynamic libraries
        ('.so' files) that were in the archive. We need to precompile these Wasm
        binaries in `load-pyodide.js`. These paths point to the unpacked
        locations of the .so files.

    metadata
        A dictionary of metadata to be stored in the package's dist-info directory.
        The keys are the names of the metadata files and the values are the contents
        of the files.

    Returns
    -------
        If calculate_dynlibs is True, a Javascript Array of dynamic libraries.
        Otherwise, return None.

    """
    if format:
        format = get_format(format)
    if not filename and format is None:
        raise ValueError("At least one of filename and format must be provided")

    extract_path = Path(extract_dir or ".")
    filename = filename.rpartition("/")[-1]

    extract_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=filename) as f:
        buffer._into_file(f)
        shutil.unpack_archive(f.name, extract_path, format)

        suffix = Path(f.name).suffix
        if suffix == ".whl":
            z = ZipFile(f)
            if metadata:
                set_wheel_metadata(filename, z, extract_path, metadata)

            install_datafiles(filename, z, extract_path)

        if calculate_dynlibs:
            return to_js(get_dynlibs(f, suffix, extract_path))

    return None


def should_load_dynlib(path: str | Path) -> bool:
    path = Path(path)

    if not SHAREDLIB_REGEX.search(path.name):
        return False

    suffixes = path.suffixes

    try:
        tag = suffixes[suffixes.index(".so") - 1]
    except ValueError:  # This should not happen, but just in case
        return False

    if tag in EXTENSION_TAGS:
        return True
    # Okay probably it's not compatible now. But it might be an unrelated .so
    # file with a name with an extra dot: `some.name.so` vs
    # `some.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so` Let's make a best effort here to
    # check.
    return not PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX.match(tag)


def set_wheel_metadata(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
    metadata: dict[str, str],
) -> None:
    """Record the metadata of a wheel into the target directory.

    Common metadata includes the installer file according to the packaging spec:
    packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/recording-installed-packages/#the-dist-info-directory

    The packaging spec allows us to make custom files. It also allows wheels to
    include custom files in their .dist-info directory. The spec has no attempt
    to coordinate these so that installers don't trample files that wheels
    include. We make a best effort with our PYODIDE prefix.

    Parameters
    ----------
    filename
        The file name of the wheel.

    archive
        A ZipFile object representing the wheel file.

    target_dir
        The directory the wheel is being installed into. Probably site-packages.

    installer
        The name of the installer. Currently either `pyodide.unpackArchive`,
        `pyodide.loadPackage` or `micropip`.

    source
        Where did the package come from? Either a url, `pyodide`, or `PyPI`.
    """
    wheel_name = parse_wheel_name(filename)[0]
    dist_info_name = wheel_dist_info_dir(archive, wheel_name)
    dist_info = target_dir / dist_info_name
    for key, value in metadata.items():
        (dist_info / key).write_text(value)


def install_datafiles(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
) -> None:
    """
    Install data files from a wheel into the target directory.
    While data files are not standard in wheels, they are common in the wild and pip supports them.
    """

    wheel_name = parse_wheel_name(filename)[0]
    data_file_dir_name = wheel_data_file_dir(archive, wheel_name)
    if data_file_dir_name is None:
        return

    data_file_dir = target_dir / data_file_dir_name / DATA_FILES_SCHEME
    if not data_file_dir.exists():
        return
    install_files(data_file_dir, sys.prefix)


def get_dynlibs(archive: IO[bytes], suffix: str, target_dir: Path) -> list[str]:
    """List out the paths to .so files in a zip or tar archive.

    Parameters
    ----------
    archive
        A binary representation of either a zip or a tar archive. We use the `.name`
        field to determine which file type.

    target_dir
        The directory the archive is unpacked into. Paths will be adjusted to point
        inside this directory.

    Returns
    -------
        The list of paths to dynamic libraries ('.so' files) that were in the archive,
        but adjusted to point to their unpacked locations.
    """
    import tarfile

    dynlib_paths_iter: Iterable[str]
    if suffix in ZIP_TYPES:
        dynlib_paths_iter = ZipFile(archive).namelist()
    elif suffix in TAR_TYPES:
        dynlib_paths_iter = (tinfo.name for tinfo in tarfile.open(archive.name))
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unexpected suffix {suffix}")

    return [
        str((target_dir / path).resolve())
        for path in dynlib_paths_iter
        if should_load_dynlib(path)
    ]


def get_dist_source(dist_path: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Get the package name and a description of the source of a package.

    This is used in loadPackage to explain where the package came from. Purely
    for informative purposes.
    """
    with (dist_path / "METADATA").open() as f:
        for line in f:
            if line.startswith("Name:"):
                dist_name = line[5:].strip()
                break
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Package name not found in {dist_path.name} METADATA")

    source_path = dist_path / PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE
    if source_path.exists():
        source = source_path.read_text().strip()
        if source == "pyodide":
            return dist_name, "default channel"
        elif source:
            return dist_name, source
    direct_url_path = dist_path / "direct_url.json"
    if direct_url_path.exists():
        import json

        return dist_name, json.loads(direct_url_path.read_text())["url"]
    installer_path = dist_path / "INSTALLER"
    if installer_path.exists():
        installer = installer_path.read_text().strip()
        return dist_name, f"{installer} (index unknown)"
    return dist_name, "Unknown"


def init_loaded_packages() -> None:
    """Initialize pyodide.loadedPackages with the packages that are already
    present.

    This ensures that `pyodide.loadPackage` knows that they are around and
    doesn't install over them.
    """
    for dist_path in SITE_PACKAGES.glob("*.dist-info"):
        dist_name, dist_source = get_dist_source(dist_path)
        setattr(loadedPackages, dist_name, dist_source)

### File Overview
This file provides helper functions for unpacking and installing Python package archives (wheels, zips, tars) into the Pyodide environment, handling wheel metadata, discovering dynamic libraries (.so files), and initializing the in-browser loadedPackages mapping.

### Architecture
- Global configuration/constants define site paths and archive suffix/type recognition (SITE_PACKAGES, DSO_DIR, TARGETS, ZIP_TYPES, TAR_TYPES, EXTENSION_TAGS, PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX, SHAREDLIB_REGEX, etc.).
- Archive format registration (make_whlfile + shutil.register_* when IN_BROWSER) integrates wheel handling into shutil.
- Utility functions parse wheel filenames and canonicalize package names.
- Wheel/mapping helpers inspect ZipFile archives to find .dist-info and .data directories and to write/read metadata files.
- unpack_buffer is the main unpack-and-install entrypoint used by callers (e.g., loadPackage), with options to calculate dynamic libs and write provided metadata into the installed dist-info.
- get_dynlibs and should_load_dynlib determine which .so files need precompilation/loading.
- get_dist_source and init_loaded_packages read installed dist-info to populate the JS-visible loadedPackages mapping (pyodide_js.loadedPackages).

These components interact as: incoming archive buffers are written to a temp file by unpack_buffer, which uses shutil.unpack_archive and then wheel-specific helpers (set_wheel_metadata, install_datafiles) and optionally get_dynlibs. Installed .dist-info files are read by get_dist_source and used by init_loaded_packages to populate loadedPackages.

### Functions & Classes
- SITE_PACKAGES — Path(getsitepackages()[0]) — primary installation directory for site packages. Important as default install target.
- DSO_DIR — computed path for dynamic libraries depending on whether running in a virtualenv. Used as TARGETS["dynlib"].
- TARGETS — {"site": SITE_PACKAGES, "dynlib": DSO_DIR} — map for get_install_dir.
- ZIP_TYPES — set of zip-like suffixes (".whl", ".zip") used by get_dynlibs to detect zip archives.
- TAR_TYPES — set of tar-like suffixes used by get_dynlibs to detect tar archives.
- EXTENSION_TAGS — list derived from importlib.machinery.EXTENSION_SUFFIXES, used when deciding compatibility of .so filenames.
- PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX — regex to detect platform tags in shared library filenames; used by should_load_dynlib to decide if a .so is platform-tagged.
- SHAREDLIB_REGEX — regex to detect ".so" in filenames; used by should_load_dynlib.
- DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX — ".dist-info" — suffix for dist-info directories used throughout.
- DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX — ".data" — suffix for data files dir in wheels.
- DATA_FILES_SCHEME — "data" — scheme name appended to data files dir.
- PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE — "PYODIDE_SOURCE" — filename used to store a package's source string in dist-info; used by set_wheel_metadata and get_dist_source.
- loadedPackages (imported from pyodide_js or None) — JS-visible object that maps installed package names to their source description; used by init_loaded_packages to initialize JS state.

- parse_wheel_name(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str, str, str, str] — splits a wheel filename into (name, version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform). Raises ValueError for invalid names. Important for locating corresponding .dist-info and .data directories and for naming consistency.

- canonicalize_name(name: str) -> str — returns the PEP 503 canonicalized name (replace [-_.]+ with "-" and lowercased). Used to compare wheel metadata directory names against expected canonical names.

- UnsupportedWheel (class) — Exception subclass to indicate unsupported or malformed wheels. Raised by wheel_dist_info_dir in mismatch cases.

- find_wheel_metadata_dir(source: ZipFile, suffix: str) -> str | None — Scans ZipFile.namelist() to find a top-level directory ending with the given suffix (e.g., ".dist-info" or ".data") and returns the first match or None. Important for both dist-info and data file discovery.

- wheel_dist_info_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str — Returns the dist-info directory name inside a wheel and validates it starts with the canonicalized wheel name. Raises UnsupportedWheel if not found or mismatch. Important for correct dist-info handling and to avoid installing incompatible wheels.

- wheel_data_file_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str | None — Returns the data-file directory name inside a wheel if present and if it matches the canonicalized name; returns None if missing or mismatched. Used by install_datafiles.

- make_whlfile(*args, owner: int | None = None, group: int | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> str — Thin wrapper around shutil._make_zipfile to create a wheel archive; registered as the "whl" archive format in IN_BROWSER. Important for browser-side archive creation support.

- get_format(format: str) -> str — Resolves a format token or extension to the canonical shutil format name by inspecting shutil.get_unpack_formats(). Raises ValueError for unknown formats. Used by unpack_buffer to canonicalize the requested/derived format string.

- get_install_dir(target: Literal["site", "dynlib"] | None = None) -> str — Returns the installation directory path for given target; default SITE_PACKAGES. Simple helper used to determine where to install.

- unpack_buffer(
    buffer: JsBuffer,
    *,
    filename: str = "",
    format: str | None = None,
    extract_dir: str | None = None,
    calculate_dynlibs: bool = False,
    metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> JsArray[str] | None — Main unpack-and-install helper invoked by higher-level callers (not in this file). Behavior:
  - Requires at least filename or format.
  - Writes buffer to a NamedTemporaryFile, then calls shutil.unpack_archive into extract_dir.
  - If the temp file suffix is ".whl", opens ZipFile and:
    - If metadata provided, calls set_wheel_metadata to write metadata files into the wheel's dist-info in target_dir.
    - Calls install_datafiles to install the wheel's .data/data scheme files, if present.
  - If calculate_dynlibs True, calls get_dynlibs and returns a JsArray of dynlib paths (converted via to_js).
  - Otherwise returns None.
  Importance: central entrypoint for installing archives and optionally computing dynamic libraries needed for subsequent precompilation/loading; used by loadPackage and possibly micropip integration.

- should_load_dynlib(path: str | Path) -> bool — Determines whether a given path (string or Path) represents an extension .so that should be considered for dynamic loading:
  - Returns False if name doesn't contain ".so".
  - Looks at suffixes to find the token before ".so" and returns True if that token matches known EXTENSION_TAGS.
  - Falls back to checking PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX to avoid loading unrelated .so files that lack extension tags.
  Importance: filters which .so files are returned by get_dynlibs and therefore which need special handling/precompilation.

- set_wheel_metadata(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
    metadata: dict[str, str],
) -> None — Writes key->value pairs (metadata) into the wheel's dist-info directory under target_dir. Uses parse_wheel_name and wheel_dist_info_dir to find the correct dist-info dir and writes files there. Important because it records installer/source info into installed packages; notably writes the PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE when callers include that key/value.

- install_datafiles(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
) -> None — If the wheel contains a .data/<DATA_FILES_SCHEME> directory matching the package, calls install_files(data_file_dir, sys.prefix). Important for supporting non-code data-files included in wheels.

- get_dynlibs(archive: IO[bytes], suffix: str, target_dir: Path) -> list[str] — Scans a zip or tar archive to list paths that should be considered dynamic libraries (.so files) and returns their resolved paths adjusted into target_dir. Uses should_load_dynlib for selection. Raises ValueError for unexpected suffix. Important because callers (e.g., unpack_buffer with calculate_dynlibs True) rely on this list to precompile/load native extensions.

- get_dist_source(dist_path: Path) -> tuple[str, str] — Reads the installed distribution's METADATA to extract the package name ("Name:" field). Then probes:
  - dist_path / PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE (reads text; returns "default channel" if contents == "pyodide"; otherwise returns the custom string)
  - dist_path / "direct_url.json" (if exists, parses JSON and returns its "url")
  - dist_path / "INSTALLER" (reads installer name and returns f"{installer} (index unknown)")
  - otherwise returns "Unknown"

## File: src/py/pyodide/_package_loader.py

## Agent's reason for reading this file:

## Source Code:
import re
import shutil
import sys
from collections.abc import Iterable
from importlib.machinery import EXTENSION_SUFFIXES
from pathlib import Path
from site import getsitepackages
from tempfile import NamedTemporaryFile
from typing import IO, Any, Literal
from zipfile import ZipFile

try:
    from pyodide_js import loadedPackages
except ImportError:
    loadedPackages = None

from .common import install_files
from .ffi import IN_BROWSER, JsArray, JsBuffer, to_js

SITE_PACKAGES = Path(getsitepackages()[0])
if sys.base_prefix == sys.prefix:
    # not in a virtualenv
    DSO_DIR = Path("/usr/lib")
else:
    # in a virtualenv
    # Better not put stuff into /usr/lib or /lib/python3.10! Stick the stdlib
    # into SITE_PACKAGES and dsos up two directories.
    #
    # e.g., SITE_PACKAGES = .venv/lib/python3.10/site_packages
    # and   DSO_DIR       = .venv/lib/
    DSO_DIR = SITE_PACKAGES.parents[1]
TARGETS = {"site": SITE_PACKAGES, "dynlib": DSO_DIR}

ZIP_TYPES = {".whl", ".zip"}
TAR_TYPES = {
    ".bz",
    ".bz2",
    ".tbz2",
    ".gz",
    ".tgz",
    ".tar",
}
EXTENSION_TAGS = [suffix.removesuffix(".so") for suffix in EXTENSION_SUFFIXES]
# See PEP 3149. I think the situation has since been updated since PEP 3149 does
# not talk about platform triples. But I could not find any newer pep discussing
# shared library names.
#
# There are other interpreters but it's better to have false negatives than
# false positives.
PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX = re.compile(
    r"\.(cpython|pypy|jython)-[0-9]{2,}[a-z]*(-[a-z0-9_-]*)?"
)
SHAREDLIB_REGEX = re.compile(r"\.so(.\d+)*$")

DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX = ".dist-info"
DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX = ".data"
# There are other "scheme"s available, but we are not interested in them.
# https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/81041f7f573e89361e6ed934436adb6bf40ea3bc/src/pip/_internal/models/scheme.py#L10
DATA_FILES_SCHEME = "data"

PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE = "PYODIDE_SOURCE"


def parse_wheel_name(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str, str, str, str]:
    tokens = filename.split("-")
    # TODO: support optional build tags in the filename (cf PEP 427)
    if len(tokens) < 5:
        raise ValueError(f"{filename} is not a valid wheel file name.")
    version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform = tokens[-4:]
    name = "-".join(tokens[:-4])
    return name, version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform


# Vendored from packaging
_canonicalize_regex = re.compile(r"[-_.]+")


def canonicalize_name(name: str) -> str:
    # This is taken from PEP 503.
    return _canonicalize_regex.sub("-", name).lower()


# Vendored from pip
class UnsupportedWheel(Exception):
    """Unsupported wheel."""


def find_wheel_metadata_dir(source: ZipFile, suffix: str) -> str | None:
    """
    Returns the name of the contained metadata directory inside the wheel file.

    Parameters
    ----------
    source
        A ZipFile object representing the wheel file.

    suffix
        The suffix of the metadata directory. Usually ".dist-info" or ".data"

    Returns
    -------
        The name of the metadata directory. If not found, returns None.
    """

    # Zip file path separators must be /
    subdirs = {p.split("/", 1)[0] for p in source.namelist()}

    info_dirs = [s for s in subdirs if s.endswith(suffix)]

    if not info_dirs:
        return None

    # Choose the first directory if there are multiple directories
    info_dir = info_dirs[0]
    return info_dir


def wheel_dist_info_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str:
    """
    Returns the name of the contained .dist-info directory.
    """
    dist_info_dir = find_wheel_metadata_dir(source, suffix=DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX)
    if dist_info_dir is None:
        raise UnsupportedWheel(
            f"{DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX} directory not found in wheel {name!r}"
        )

    dist_info_dir_name = canonicalize_name(dist_info_dir)
    canonical_name = canonicalize_name(name)
    if not dist_info_dir_name.startswith(canonical_name):
        raise UnsupportedWheel(
            f"{DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX} directory {dist_info_dir!r} does not start with {canonical_name!r}"
        )

    return dist_info_dir


def wheel_data_file_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str | None:
    data_file_dir = find_wheel_metadata_dir(source, suffix=DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX)

    # data files are optional, so we return None if not found
    if data_file_dir is None:
        return None

    data_file_dir_name = canonicalize_name(data_file_dir)
    canonical_name = canonicalize_name(name)
    if not data_file_dir_name.startswith(canonical_name):
        return None

    return data_file_dir


def make_whlfile(
    *args: Any, owner: int | None = None, group: int | None = None, **kwargs: Any
) -> str:
    return shutil._make_zipfile(*args, **kwargs)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]


if IN_BROWSER:
    shutil.register_archive_format("whl", make_whlfile, description="Wheel file")
    shutil.register_unpack_format(
        "whl",
        [".whl", ".wheel"],
        shutil._unpack_zipfile,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        description="Wheel file",
    )


def get_format(format: str) -> str:
    for fmt, extensions, _ in shutil.get_unpack_formats():
        if format == fmt:
            return fmt
        if format in extensions:
            return fmt
        if "." + format in extensions:
            return fmt
    raise ValueError(f"Unrecognized format {format}")


def get_install_dir(target: Literal["site", "dynlib"] | None = None) -> str:
    """
    Get the installation directory for a target.
    """
    if not target:
        return str(SITE_PACKAGES)

    return str(TARGETS.get(target, SITE_PACKAGES))


def unpack_buffer(
    buffer: JsBuffer,
    *,
    filename: str = "",
    format: str | None = None,
    extract_dir: str | None = None,
    calculate_dynlibs: bool = False,
    metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> JsArray[str] | None:
    """Used to install a package either into sitepackages or into the standard
    library.

    This is a helper method called from ``loadPackage``.

    Parameters
    ----------
    buffer
        A Javascript ``Uint8Array`` with the binary data for the archive.

    filename
        The name of the file we are extracting. We only care about it to figure
        out whether the buffer represents a tar file or a zip file. Ignored if
        format argument is present.

    format
        Controls the format that we assume the archive has. Overrides the file
        extension of filename. In particular we decide the file format as
        follows:

        1. If format is present, we use that.
        2. If file name is present, it should have an extension, like a.zip,
           a.tar, etc. Then we use that.
        3. If neither is present or the file name has no extension, we throw an
           error.

    extract_dir
        Controls which directory the file is unpacked into. Default is the
        working directory.

    calculate_dynlibs
        If true, will return a Javascript Array of paths to dynamic libraries
        ('.so' files) that were in the archive. We need to precompile these Wasm
        binaries in `load-pyodide.js`. These paths point to the unpacked
        locations of the .so files.

    metadata
        A dictionary of metadata to be stored in the package's dist-info directory.
        The keys are the names of the metadata files and the values are the contents
        of the files.

    Returns
    -------
        If calculate_dynlibs is True, a Javascript Array of dynamic libraries.
        Otherwise, return None.

    """
    if format:
        format = get_format(format)
    if not filename and format is None:
        raise ValueError("At least one of filename and format must be provided")

    extract_path = Path(extract_dir or ".")
    filename = filename.rpartition("/")[-1]

    extract_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    with NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=filename) as f:
        buffer._into_file(f)
        shutil.unpack_archive(f.name, extract_path, format)

        suffix = Path(f.name).suffix
        if suffix == ".whl":
            z = ZipFile(f)
            if metadata:
                set_wheel_metadata(filename, z, extract_path, metadata)

            install_datafiles(filename, z, extract_path)

        if calculate_dynlibs:
            return to_js(get_dynlibs(f, suffix, extract_path))

    return None


def should_load_dynlib(path: str | Path) -> bool:
    path = Path(path)

    if not SHAREDLIB_REGEX.search(path.name):
        return False

    suffixes = path.suffixes

    try:
        tag = suffixes[suffixes.index(".so") - 1]
    except ValueError:  # This should not happen, but just in case
        return False

    if tag in EXTENSION_TAGS:
        return True
    # Okay probably it's not compatible now. But it might be an unrelated .so
    # file with a name with an extra dot: `some.name.so` vs
    # `some.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so` Let's make a best effort here to
    # check.
    return not PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX.match(tag)


def set_wheel_metadata(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
    metadata: dict[str, str],
) -> None:
    """Record the metadata of a wheel into the target directory.

    Common metadata includes the installer file according to the packaging spec:
    packaging.python.org/en/latest/specifications/recording-installed-packages/#the-dist-info-directory

    The packaging spec allows us to make custom files. It also allows wheels to
    include custom files in their .dist-info directory. The spec has no attempt
    to coordinate these so that installers don't trample files that wheels
    include. We make a best effort with our PYODIDE prefix.

    Parameters
    ----------
    filename
        The file name of the wheel.

    archive
        A ZipFile object representing the wheel file.

    target_dir
        The directory the wheel is being installed into. Probably site-packages.

    installer
        The name of the installer. Currently either `pyodide.unpackArchive`,
        `pyodide.loadPackage` or `micropip`.

    source
        Where did the package come from? Either a url, `pyodide`, or `PyPI`.
    """
    wheel_name = parse_wheel_name(filename)[0]
    dist_info_name = wheel_dist_info_dir(archive, wheel_name)
    dist_info = target_dir / dist_info_name
    for key, value in metadata.items():
        (dist_info / key).write_text(value)


def install_datafiles(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
) -> None:
    """
    Install data files from a wheel into the target directory.
    While data files are not standard in wheels, they are common in the wild and pip supports them.
    """

    wheel_name = parse_wheel_name(filename)[0]
    data_file_dir_name = wheel_data_file_dir(archive, wheel_name)
    if data_file_dir_name is None:
        return

    data_file_dir = target_dir / data_file_dir_name / DATA_FILES_SCHEME
    if not data_file_dir.exists():
        return
    install_files(data_file_dir, sys.prefix)


def get_dynlibs(archive: IO[bytes], suffix: str, target_dir: Path) -> list[str]:
    """List out the paths to .so files in a zip or tar archive.

    Parameters
    ----------
    archive
        A binary representation of either a zip or a tar archive. We use the `.name`
        field to determine which file type.

    target_dir
        The directory the archive is unpacked into. Paths will be adjusted to point
        inside this directory.

    Returns
    -------
        The list of paths to dynamic libraries ('.so' files) that were in the archive,
        but adjusted to point to their unpacked locations.
    """
    import tarfile

    dynlib_paths_iter: Iterable[str]
    if suffix in ZIP_TYPES:
        dynlib_paths_iter = ZipFile(archive).namelist()
    elif suffix in TAR_TYPES:
        dynlib_paths_iter = (tinfo.name for tinfo in tarfile.open(archive.name))
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Unexpected suffix {suffix}")

    return [
        str((target_dir / path).resolve())
        for path in dynlib_paths_iter
        if should_load_dynlib(path)
    ]


def get_dist_source(dist_path: Path) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Get the package name and a description of the source of a package.

    This is used in loadPackage to explain where the package came from. Purely
    for informative purposes.
    """
    with (dist_path / "METADATA").open() as f:
        for line in f:
            if line.startswith("Name:"):
                dist_name = line[5:].strip()
                break
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"Package name not found in {dist_path.name} METADATA")

    source_path = dist_path / PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE
    if source_path.exists():
        source = source_path.read_text().strip()
        if source == "pyodide":
            return dist_name, "default channel"
        elif source:
            return dist_name, source
    direct_url_path = dist_path / "direct_url.json"
    if direct_url_path.exists():
        import json

        return dist_name, json.loads(direct_url_path.read_text())["url"]
    installer_path = dist_path / "INSTALLER"
    if installer_path.exists():
        installer = installer_path.read_text().strip()
        return dist_name, f"{installer} (index unknown)"
    return dist_name, "Unknown"


def init_loaded_packages() -> None:
    """Initialize pyodide.loadedPackages with the packages that are already
    present.

    This ensures that `pyodide.loadPackage` knows that they are around and
    doesn't install over them.
    """
    for dist_path in SITE_PACKAGES.glob("*.dist-info"):
        dist_name, dist_source = get_dist_source(dist_path)
        setattr(loadedPackages, dist_name, dist_source)
summarization.summary:
### File Overview
This file provides helper functions for unpacking and installing Python package archives (wheels, zips, tars) into the Pyodide environment, handling wheel metadata, discovering dynamic libraries (.so files), and initializing the in-browser loadedPackages mapping.

### Architecture
- Global configuration/constants define site paths and archive suffix/type recognition (SITE_PACKAGES, DSO_DIR, TARGETS, ZIP_TYPES, TAR_TYPES, EXTENSION_TAGS, PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX, SHAREDLIB_REGEX, etc.).
- Archive format registration (make_whlfile + shutil.register_* when IN_BROWSER) integrates wheel handling into shutil.
- Utility functions parse wheel filenames and canonicalize package names.
- Wheel/mapping helpers inspect ZipFile archives to find .dist-info and .data directories and to write/read metadata files.
- unpack_buffer is the main unpack-and-install entrypoint used by callers (e.g., loadPackage), with options to calculate dynamic libs and write provided metadata into the installed dist-info.
- get_dynlibs and should_load_dynlib determine which .so files need precompilation/loading.
- get_dist_source and init_loaded_packages read installed dist-info to populate the JS-visible loadedPackages mapping (pyodide_js.loadedPackages).

These components interact as: incoming archive buffers are written to a temp file by unpack_buffer, which uses shutil.unpack_archive and then wheel-specific helpers (set_wheel_metadata, install_datafiles) and optionally get_dynlibs. Installed .dist-info files are read by get_dist_source and used by init_loaded_packages to populate loadedPackages.

### Functions & Classes
- SITE_PACKAGES — Path(getsitepackages()[0]) — primary installation directory for site packages. Important as default install target.
- DSO_DIR — computed path for dynamic libraries depending on whether running in a virtualenv. Used as TARGETS["dynlib"].
- TARGETS — {"site": SITE_PACKAGES, "dynlib": DSO_DIR} — map for get_install_dir.
- ZIP_TYPES — set of zip-like suffixes (".whl", ".zip") used by get_dynlibs to detect zip archives.
- TAR_TYPES — set of tar-like suffixes used by get_dynlibs to detect tar archives.
- EXTENSION_TAGS — list derived from importlib.machinery.EXTENSION_SUFFIXES, used when deciding compatibility of .so filenames.
- PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX — regex to detect platform tags in shared library filenames; used by should_load_dynlib to decide if a .so is platform-tagged.
- SHAREDLIB_REGEX — regex to detect ".so" in filenames; used by should_load_dynlib.
- DIST_INFO_DIR_SUFFIX — ".dist-info" — suffix for dist-info directories used throughout.
- DATA_FILES_DIR_SUFFIX — ".data" — suffix for data files dir in wheels.
- DATA_FILES_SCHEME — "data" — scheme name appended to data files dir.
- PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE — "PYODIDE_SOURCE" — filename used to store a package's source string in dist-info; used by set_wheel_metadata and get_dist_source.
- loadedPackages (imported from pyodide_js or None) — JS-visible object that maps installed package names to their source description; used by init_loaded_packages to initialize JS state.

- parse_wheel_name(filename: str) -> tuple[str, str, str, str, str] — splits a wheel filename into (name, version, python_tag, abi_tag, platform). Raises ValueError for invalid names. Important for locating corresponding .dist-info and .data directories and for naming consistency.

- canonicalize_name(name: str) -> str — returns the PEP 503 canonicalized name (replace [-_.]+ with "-" and lowercased). Used to compare wheel metadata directory names against expected canonical names.

- UnsupportedWheel (class) — Exception subclass to indicate unsupported or malformed wheels. Raised by wheel_dist_info_dir in mismatch cases.

- find_wheel_metadata_dir(source: ZipFile, suffix: str) -> str | None — Scans ZipFile.namelist() to find a top-level directory ending with the given suffix (e.g., ".dist-info" or ".data") and returns the first match or None. Important for both dist-info and data file discovery.

- wheel_dist_info_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str — Returns the dist-info directory name inside a wheel and validates it starts with the canonicalized wheel name. Raises UnsupportedWheel if not found or mismatch. Important for correct dist-info handling and to avoid installing incompatible wheels.

- wheel_data_file_dir(source: ZipFile, name: str) -> str | None — Returns the data-file directory name inside a wheel if present and if it matches the canonicalized name; returns None if missing or mismatched. Used by install_datafiles.

- make_whlfile(*args, owner: int | None = None, group: int | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> str — Thin wrapper around shutil._make_zipfile to create a wheel archive; registered as the "whl" archive format in IN_BROWSER. Important for browser-side archive creation support.

- get_format(format: str) -> str — Resolves a format token or extension to the canonical shutil format name by inspecting shutil.get_unpack_formats(). Raises ValueError for unknown formats. Used by unpack_buffer to canonicalize the requested/derived format string.

- get_install_dir(target: Literal["site", "dynlib"] | None = None) -> str — Returns the installation directory path for given target; default SITE_PACKAGES. Simple helper used to determine where to install.

- unpack_buffer(
    buffer: JsBuffer,
    *,
    filename: str = "",
    format: str | None = None,
    extract_dir: str | None = None,
    calculate_dynlibs: bool = False,
    metadata: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> JsArray[str] | None — Main unpack-and-install helper invoked by higher-level callers (not in this file). Behavior:
  - Requires at least filename or format.
  - Writes buffer to a NamedTemporaryFile, then calls shutil.unpack_archive into extract_dir.
  - If the temp file suffix is ".whl", opens ZipFile and:
    - If metadata provided, calls set_wheel_metadata to write metadata files into the wheel's dist-info in target_dir.
    - Calls install_datafiles to install the wheel's .data/data scheme files, if present.
  - If calculate_dynlibs True, calls get_dynlibs and returns a JsArray of dynlib paths (converted via to_js).
  - Otherwise returns None.
  Importance: central entrypoint for installing archives and optionally computing dynamic libraries needed for subsequent precompilation/loading; used by loadPackage and possibly micropip integration.

- should_load_dynlib(path: str | Path) -> bool — Determines whether a given path (string or Path) represents an extension .so that should be considered for dynamic loading:
  - Returns False if name doesn't contain ".so".
  - Looks at suffixes to find the token before ".so" and returns True if that token matches known EXTENSION_TAGS.
  - Falls back to checking PLATFORM_TAG_REGEX to avoid loading unrelated .so files that lack extension tags.
  Importance: filters which .so files are returned by get_dynlibs and therefore which need special handling/precompilation.

- set_wheel_metadata(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
    metadata: dict[str, str],
) -> None — Writes key->value pairs (metadata) into the wheel's dist-info directory under target_dir. Uses parse_wheel_name and wheel_dist_info_dir to find the correct dist-info dir and writes files there. Important because it records installer/source info into installed packages; notably writes the PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE when callers include that key/value.

- install_datafiles(
    filename: str,
    archive: ZipFile,
    target_dir: Path,
) -> None — If the wheel contains a .data/<DATA_FILES_SCHEME> directory matching the package, calls install_files(data_file_dir, sys.prefix). Important for supporting non-code data-files included in wheels.

- get_dynlibs(archive: IO[bytes], suffix: str, target_dir: Path) -> list[str] — Scans a zip or tar archive to list paths that should be considered dynamic libraries (.so files) and returns their resolved paths adjusted into target_dir. Uses should_load_dynlib for selection. Raises ValueError for unexpected suffix. Important because callers (e.g., unpack_buffer with calculate_dynlibs True) rely on this list to precompile/load native extensions.

- get_dist_source(dist_path: Path) -> tuple[str, str] — Reads the installed distribution's METADATA to extract the package name ("Name:" field). Then probes:
  - dist_path / PYODIDE_SOURCE_METADATA_FILE (reads text; returns "default channel" if contents == "pyodide"; otherwise returns the custom string)
  - dist_path / "direct_url.json" (if exists, parses JSON and returns its "url")
  - dist_path / "INSTALLER" (reads installer name and returns f"{installer} (index unknown)")
  - otherwise returns "Unknown"
  Returns (dist_name, source_description). Important for producing human-readable descriptions of where packages came from; used by init_loaded_packages to populate loadedPackages.

- init_loaded_packages() -> None — Iterates SITE_PACKAGES.glob("*.dist-info"), calls get_dist_source on each dist-info dir, and sets attributes on loadedPackages (setattr(loadedPackages, dist_name, dist_source)). Purpose: initialize pyodide.loadedPackages with the packages already present in the environment so loadPackage and other code won't try to re-install them. Highly relevant for tests that inspect loadedPackages state or package provenance.

### File Overview
This conftest.py provides common pytest configuration and utilities for the test suite, especially for Pyodide/browser-based tests. It sets up global test flags and initialization scripts for different browsers, registers pytest hooks, defines test-selection helpers, and provides runtime checks (memory/refcount/leak checks) around tests that use selenium-based browser fixtures.

### Architecture
- Top-level module setup: modifies sys.path, sets environment variable IN_PYTEST, imports pytest_pyodide helpers (with side effects), and defines constants PYODIDE_ROOT and DIST_PATH.
- Configuration function set_configs constructs and applies browser-specific flags, JavaScript initialization scripts, and loadPyodide snippets to the global pytest_pyodide configuration (via get_global_config()).
- Pytest CLI extension: pytest_addoption adds CLI options.
- Test skipping logic: maybe_skip_test and pytest_collection_modifyitems decide whether to skip tests based on package build status and previous run results (cache).
- Pytest runtime hooks: pytest_configure monkey-patches test naming output and stores dist dir; pytest_terminal_summary writes per-test results to the cache; pytest_runtest_call wraps test execution to run extra leak/refcount checks for browser tests.
- Leak/refcount verification: extra_checks_test_wrapper is the generator wrapper used by pytest_runtest_call to enable/disable tracing around a test and assert no leaks.
- Utilities: package_is_built wraps a pytest_pyodide helper; strip_assertions_stderr filters known spurious stderr messages.

Components interact primarily via pytest hook APIs (e.g., pytest_runtest_call, pytest_collection_modifyitems, pytest_terminal_summary), global pytest_pyodide_config (set in set_configs), and a test-result cache key "cache/lasttestresult" saved/loaded by pytest_terminal_summary/pytest_collection_modifyitems.

### Functions & Classes
- `PYODIDE_ROOT` — pathlib.Path pointing to the repository root (parent of this file). Used to compute paths and update sys.path.
- `DIST_PATH` — path to the "dist" directory under PYODIDE_ROOT. Convenience constant; not heavily used here.
- `set_configs` — configures the pytest_pyodide global config by:
  - setting Chrome flags to include "--enable-features=WebAssemblyExperimentalJSPI",
  - setting Node flags to include "--experimental-wasm-stack-switching",
  - setting an initialization JS script (set_initialize_script) that touches several pyodide internals to avoid refcount issues,
  - setting browser-specific load_pyodide scripts for "chrome" and "node".
  Importance: central place where browser startup behavior and initialization side effects are defined; these scripts will run inside the browser environment and can produce console output.
- `only_node` — pytest mark created via pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(...). A reusable marker for tests expected to only run on node. Important for test selection/xfail behavior.
- `only_chrome` — pytest mark created via pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(...). A marker for tests expected to only run on chrome.
- `requires_jspi` — pytest mark created via pytest.mark.xfail_browsers(...). Marker for tests requiring JSPI support.
- `pytest_addoption` — pytest hook that adds two CLI options:
  - `--run-xfail` to run tests marked xfail,
  - `--skip-passed` to skip tests that passed on the last run.
  Importance: exposes CLI switches that change collection/selection behavior.
- `maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=False)` — decides whether to skip an individual test item early to avoid expensive fixtures. Behavior:
  - Determines if the test is part of a package under "packages/<name>/test_*.py".
  - If so, and the package is not built (via package_is_built), and the test name indicates a browser param that matches firefox|chrome|node|safari, then skip the test (immediately via pytest.skip if delayed is False, or add a skip marker if delayed is True).
  Importance: prevents launching heavy selenium fixtures for package tests that are not built.
- `pytest_configure(config)` — pytest hook that:
  - Monkey-patches config.cwd_relative_nodeid to shorten test identifiers printed in pytest summary tables (removes "src/tests/", "packages/", and "::test_" patterns).
  - Stores the `--dist-dir` option into `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir`.
  Importance: affects test name output (verbosity/readability) and provides the distribution directory used by package_is_built.
- `pytest_collection_modifyitems(config, items)` — pytest hook called after collection:
  - If `--skip-passed` is set, reads the previous run results from config.cache under "cache/lasttestresult".
  - For each collected item, if it previously passed, marks it skipped.
  - Also calls maybe_skip_test(item, delayed=True) to add skip markers for package-not-built cases.
  Importance: influences which tests are run and can avoid re-running previously passed tests or running unbuilt package tests.
- `pytest_terminal_summary(terminalreporter)` — pytest hook (trylast=True) used to save test outcomes to cache:
  - Iterates terminalreporter.stats, records test.call outcomes (ignores warnings/deselected).
  - Stores mapping to config.cache under "cache/lasttestresult".
  - Attempts to map skipped-as-previously-passed to "skip_passed" if detected via longrepr content.
  Importance: persistence of test outcomes to later support --skip-passed behavior.
- `pytest_runtest_call(item)` — pytest hook (wrapper=True) that:
  - Looks through fixture names for an argument whose name starts with "selenium" to find the selenium browser fixture (assigns to local variable `browser`).
  - If no browser fixture found or browser.pyodide_loaded is falsy, yields control to run the test normally and returns result.
  - Otherwise, determines tracing flags based on test markers: disables refcount tracing if test is marked `skip_pyproxy_check`, etc.
  - Delegates to `extra_checks_test_wrapper(browser, trace_hiwire_refs, trace_pyproxies, item)` by yielding from that generator wrapper so the extra checks are run around the test call.
  Importance: key hook to run extra leak/refcount checks around tests that use a selenium browser/pyodide environment.
- `extra_checks_test_wrapper(browser, trace_hiwire_refs, trace_pyproxies, item)` — wrapper generator that performs these steps around the yielded test execution:
  - Calls `browser.clear_force_test_fail()`.
  - Records `init_num_keys = browser.get_num_hiwire_keys()`.
  - If `trace_pyproxies` is True:
    - calls `browser.enable_pyproxy_tracing()` and records `init_num_proxies = browser.get_num_proxies()`.
  - Yields to execute the test (result = yield).
  - In the finally block after test (with suppressed exceptions if the test raised):
    - calls `browser.disable_pyproxy_tracing()`,
    - calls `browser.restore_state()`,
    - runs JS: `pyodide._module._clear_method_call_singleton();` to clear method call singletons that would otherwise affect refcount checks.
  - After the test, checks:
    - if `browser.force_test_fail` is set, raises an Exception indicating an explicit request for failing the test.
    - If both `trace_pyproxies` and `trace_hiwire_refs` are true:
      - computes deltas for proxies and hiwire keys; if delta_proxies > 0, calls browser.run_js to extract info about allocated pyproxies and then uses pytest's capturemanager (`capman = item.config.pluginmanager.getplugin("capturemanager")` and `with capman.item_capture("call", item):`) and prints a header and each leaked proxy row via print() to the test capture before asserting no leaks.
      - asserts (delta_proxies, delta_keys) == (0, 0) or delta_keys < 0.
    - If only `trace_hiwire_refs` is true, asserts delta_keys <= 0.
  - Returns the original test result.
  Importance: implements leak detection and reporting around tests. Notably, it may print diagnostic information about leaked proxies (via print inside a captured context), which affects stdout/stderr capture for tests.
- `package_is_built(package_name)` — wrapper around pytest_pyodide.utils.package_is_built as `_package_is_built`, passing `pytest.pyodide_dist_dir`. Importance: used to decide to skip package-specific tests if package artifacts are not present.
- `strip_assertions_stderr(messages: Sequence[str]) -> list[str]` — filters known spurious stderr messages emitted when ASSERTIONS=1; removes exact message lines from the input list and returns the filtered list. Importance: utility to clean stderr messages for test assertions; relevant to tests that assert on stderr content.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is checking for test fixtures, stdout/stderr capture, and any print/log output that affects tests (especially around package index tests). Relevant points in this file:

- `pytest_runtest_call` and `extra_checks_test_wrapper` are central: they wrap tests that use selenium/browser fixtures and perform additional checks. They explicitly print leaked proxy diagnostics (print("\n" + "!" * 40), print("leaked proxies:"), and then print each row) inside a capture context via `capman.item_capture("call", item)`. This print output is intentionally captured by pytest's capturemanager and will show up in test output if a leak occurs — so these are places where stdout is emitted by the test infrastructure.
- `set_configs` calls `pytest_pyodide_config.set_initialize_script(...)` and `set_load_pyodide_script(...)` with JS snippets that run inside browser contexts at initialization. Those scripts may produce console output from the browser that could surface in captured logs or test output depending on capture configuration.
- `strip_assertions_stderr` is provided to filter known noisy stderr lines (e.g., "sigaction: signal type not supported: this is a no-op.", "warning: no BlobBuilder"). Tests that assert on stderr should use this to ignore these lines.
- `maybe_skip_test` and `pytest_collection_modifyitems` determine whether package tests are skipped when a package is not built; they do not print but they can mark tests as skipped (which affects test run output).
- Import-time side effects: importing `get_global_config` and `use_global_safari_service` (the latter via from-import with a session-side-effect comment) and setting `os.environ["IN_PYTEST"]` can affect test behavior and any code reading that environment or using the pytest_pyodide runner.
- The use of `capman.item_capture("call", item)` indicates the code is intentionally interacting with pytest's capturemanager API to control capture during diagnostics.

If you are looking for places that produce output that could interfere with tests asserting stdout/stderr, focus on:
- The print calls in `extra_checks_test_wrapper` (they are guarded and captured but will appear when leak diagnostics are triggered).
- Any console output produced by the JavaScript snippets set in `set_configs` when loadPyodide runs in browsers (these could surface in test logs).
- The filtered stderr values in `strip_assertions_stderr` — if tests still fail due to other stderr lines, adjust filters or capture logic.
